basics.name: Milad Tatar Mamaghani
basics.summary: Research scientist and engineer working on wireless communications, physical-layer security, and AI-native 6G systems.

work.name: Independent Consultant
work.summary: Remote consulting and expert evaluation work for advanced STEM and AI tasks.
highlights: Drive research-based projects through technical evaluation and domain-specific model reasoning for top-tier AI labs.
Benchmark AI models for complex algorithmic reasoning and mathematical accuracy in STEM contexts.
Curate high-fidelity training datasets to improve AI alignment, factual precision, and reasoning.
Formulate advanced prompting strategies and evaluation guidelines for next-generation AI models.

work.name: The Australian National University
work.summary: Research in physical-layer security, UAV communications, and next-generation wireless networks.
highlights: Conducted research on physical-layer security, short-packet communications, and integrated sensing and communications.
Published 3 first-author papers in Q1 IEEE transactions: 1 IEEE JSAC paper (IF 17.2) and 2 IEEE TWC papers (IF 10.4).
Presented 2 papers at flagship conferences: IEEE ICC 2024 and IEEE GLOBECOM 2023.
Received 2 best-paper awards: IEEE SPCC-TC Best Paper Award 2024 and IEEE ICC Best Paper Award 2024 (top 0.6% of 2,364 submissions).

work.name: Monash University
work.summary: Doctoral research on physical-layer security for beyond-5G wireless networks using UAVs.
highlights: Conducted research on wireless communications security, resource allocation, signal processing, and IoT network design.
Designed reinforcement learning and mathematical optimization frameworks for energy-efficient airborne communications.
Developed aerial MIMO beamforming and energy-efficient trajectory planning architectures to secure UAV networks.
Published 7 peer-reviewed articles in leading journals and received 5+ awards, grants, and accolades for research excellence.
